title: An arrangement for removing liquid from a flow of natural gas in a gas pipe
abstract: An arrangement for removing liquid from a flow of natural gas in a gas pipe (2), which comprises a straight pipe section (12) with a uniform cross-section. The pipe section (12) is perforated along a substantial part of the circumference of the pipe section wall. The perforations (13) are in communication with a chamber (15) that arranged outside the inner wall of said pipe section (12). The chamber collects liquid that is flowing along the inside wall of the gas pipe (12). The liquid may be lead to a process unit, such as a scrubber through a liquid lock (11).
claims: Claims An arrangement for removing liquid from a flow of natural gas in a gas pipe (2), characterised in that said gas pipe (2) comprises a straight pipe section (12) with a uniform cross-section, said pipe section (12) being perforated along a substantial part of the circumference of the pipe section wall, said perforations (13) being in communication with a chamber (15) arranged outside an inner wall of said pipe section (12) ; said chamber collecting liquid that is flowing along the inside wall of the gas pipe (12) and out through said perforations (13); said chamber (15) being fluidly connected to a liquid tube (10); said liquid tube (10) in turn being fluidly connected to a processing unit (1 ) or receiving pipe section (24) that is capable of receiving liquid; and said liquid tube (10) having a liquid lock (1 1 ). The arrangement of claim 1 , characterised in that said perforations (13) are in the form of a plurality of openings (13) arranged in at least one circle along the wall of the pipe section (12). The arrangement of claim 1 , characterised in that said perforations (13) are in the form of at least one continuous slit arranged in a circle along the wall of the pipe section (12). The arrangement of any of the preceding claims, characterised in that said perforations (13) are flush with the inner wall of said pipe section (12). The arrangement of any of the preceding claims, characterised in that a liquid stopper (16) is arranged adjacent the perforations (13) to hold back the liquid flowing along the inside wall about the perforations (13). The arrangement of any of the preceding claims, characterised in that said processing unit (1 ) or receiving pipe section (24) has a lower pressure than the gas pipe. 7. The arrangement of any of the preceding claims, characterised in that said liquid lock (1 1 ) is in the form of a U-shaped tube section. 8. The arrangement of any of the preceding claims, characterised in that the liquid level in the liquid lock is adjusted by positioning the inlet to the liquid tube relative to the outlet from the liquid tube. 9. The arrangement of any of the preceding claims, characterised in that said gas pipe is an inlet pipe to a scrubber. 10. The arrangement of any of any of the preceding claims, characterised in that said gas pipe is an outlet gas pipe from a gas- liquid separation unit. 1 1 . The arrangement of claim 10, characterised in that said gas-liquid separation unit is a gravity separator. 12. The arrangement of any of the preceding claims, characterised in that the process unit is a scrubber. 13. The arrangement of any of the preceding claims, characterised in that said receiving pipe section is a liquid outlet pipe from a scrubber. 14. The arrangement of any of the preceding claims, characterised in that said receiving pipe is a liquid pipe downstream a gas-liquid separation unit.
